examples: A groundwater reservoir from which water can be extracted is called an aquifer.
Extraction of water depends on two properties of the aquifer: porosity and permeability.
Water does not remain immobile in an aquifer but can seep out at springs or leak into other aquifers.
Thousands of miles of pipelines run over the Ogallala aquifer, which cannot be replaced or cleaned-up.
About 2 billion people worldwide depend on groundwater supplies, which include 273 transboundary aquifer systems.
